note: Ring presets (owner spec): one entry = one dial styling. positions are dial hours (24 = the bottom Omega slot in DOMY); letters pair with positions by index and come from the letter library (RING_LETTER_FILES). The positions signature picks the LAYOUT: flame (12,20,24,4 - the gold triangle points up), chalice (12,16,24,8 - points down), seal (all six). Adding an entry here (or a custom one in Settings) is all a new styling needs. Optional 'triangle' (seal layout only, ROADMAP 15b): overrides the layout's own metal triangle so a 6-letter preset can split into two 3-letter metal groups instead of the seal's own plain single finish on all six; on Mason/Omega/Templar this override only actually APPLIES when the owner's per-preset 'Two metals' toggle is on (TASK 3, MASON/ICONS round, settings.ring_two_metals / app.controller._ring_two_metals) — off, the preset falls back to one finish on all six exactly like a card with no triangle at all. Optional 'legend': hour(as the position int) -> {name, reading} — the per-letter HOVER LEGEND (owner: what a letter stands for), quoted verbatim from CANON.md's Banknote table; each reading may carry a second '\n\n'-separated paragraph, the axis-opposition line (TASK 2, owner 'moze'). Optional 'motto' (TASK 1, owner 'moze radi'; corrected MOTO-FIX round, owner correction 2026-07-19, the dollar's Great Seal reference image; corrected ANNUIT WORD-GAP round, owner correction 2026-07-19, third batch): a list of Great Seal motto entries {text, pins, clockwise}, pins = [letter, occurrence, position] triples; optional per-entry 'clockwise' (default true) picks the arc's reading direction — true sweeps increasing angle (the TOP arc), false sweeps decreasing (the BOTTOM arc) — the outer curved-text arc outside the ring band, validated and angle-solved by data.rings.validate_preset/core.motto. RENAMES (TASK 2, MASON/ICONS round, owner verdicts 2026-07-19, third batch): 'MORPH' -> 'Morph', 'MASON G' -> 'Mason', 'NUMBERS' -> 'Omega'; app.settings_store migrates a stored settings file's old name onto the new one. New bundled preset 'Templar': the seal layout, all six positions wearing the templar-cross glyph, no motto, no legend.

12.name: G
12.reading:
God — the masonic G that means both GOD and GEOMETRY, crowning the Eye.

Across the wheel: Ω — God against the End, the Alpha who begins facing the Omega who sums the whole (Revelation 1:8).

16.name: S
16.reading:
Sigma — the union's letter on Courage's arm.

Across the wheel: N — Satan against the Nazarene, the Accuser facing the Advocate (Revelation 12:10; 1 John 2:1).

20.name: M
20.reading:
the Master — the Devil's face of mastery, on Pride's arm.

Across the wheel: A — the Master against the Angel, Pride's boss-syndrome facing the ministering spirit sent to serve (Hebrews 1:14).

24.name: Ω
24.reading:
Omega — and the dial's ring already seats Ω at 24h.

Across the wheel: G — the End against God, the Sum of all that was facing the Geometry that first ordered it.

4.name: N
4.reading:
the Nazarene — Jesus on Hope's arm.

Across the wheel: S — the Nazarene against Satan, the Advocate facing the Accuser (1 John 2:1; Revelation 12:10).

8.name: A
8.reading:
Alpha — the beginning, on Renewal's green.

Across the wheel: M — the Angel against the Master, the ministering spirit sent to serve (Hebrews 1:14) facing the one who would only be served.
